intro_text: It's not finding fresh juniper that's the problem. As an evergreen plant, juniper is known to grow reliably even in the cold seasons. The problem is that juniper grows in large shrubs; shrubs sometimes large enough to be literal trees! Climbing in these slippery conditions is a disaster just waiting to happen.

success_outcomes.text: Truly, dandelions are an essential herb to keep in stock, crucial for poison and stings. The white milk in their roots and stems keeps for a long time, and is a potent cure for poison — their leaves are weaker, but fast-acting, and can be chewed into poultices. Even the fluffy dandelion heads are useful as a c_n kitten's favorite, short-lived toy. Just as useful is the wild garlic that {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} {VERB/p_l/spot/spots} a little ways from the dandelions, hidden by some undergrowth, but impossible to miss due to their scent.

It's a successful gathering mission, and p_l strides back to camp with a great haul of entire dandelion plants, and bonus wild garlic, too. Later {PRONOUN/p_l/subject}'ll sort the leaves and roots to be stored separately, the leaves remain fresh for a far shorter time.

With warmth in the air and ground, s_c doesn't need to try very hard to find an excellent haul of dandelions to bring back to camp. It is to also {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} delight that a stinky scent tickles {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} nose as {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} start the haul back to camp and {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} track down its origin — fresh wild garlic, ready to be collected! The white milk in their roots and stems keeps for a long time, and is a potent cure for poison — their leaves are weaker, but fast acting, and can be chewed into poultices. Even the fluffy dandelion heads are useful as a c_n kitten's favorite, short-lived toy. Also as useful — but not a toy — is the wild garlic that p_l tracks down a few fox-lengths away from the dandelion patch they've found.

In small doses, it does help with nearly any ailment... but in large doses, it poisons the body, even to the point of causing death. The yew drank of their blood, and in the colder moons, even now, you can see it shining in its deathberries.

success_outcomes.text: p_l leads the apprentices along common routes, pointing out herbs along the way. Wild garlic, burdock root, even raspberry bunches can be found near the best hunting grounds or by the border. One should always keep one's eye out, though p_l reminds them never to ingest anything before it is identified by a medicine cat. The apprentices remain attentive throughout the patrol, taking in the lesson p_l offers.

log.cats_from: cat_from was impressed by cat_to's focus during an herb identification lesson.

fail_outcomes.text: p_l sighs. The patrol is going exactly how {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} expected. None of the apprentices can focus and the warrior apprentices grumble that the information is useless. For StarClan's sake, they're as immature as kits!

log.cats_from: cat_from was frustrated that cat_to seemed to dismiss {PRONOUN/cat_from/poss} advice and teaching.
log.cats_to: cat_to didn't understand why anything cat_from was trying to teach {PRONOUN/cat_to/object} was relevant.

fail_outcomes.text: As the patrol sets out, a sudden rain pours from the sky. The apprentices whine and moan about getting their pelts wet. p_l doesn't have the patience for this and turns the patrol around.

log.cats_from: cat_from was irritated that cat_to was more concerned with keeping {PRONOUN/cat_to/poss} pelt dry than with cat_from's important lesson.
